expectations.text: Explicitly names the SUCCESs principles (at least 4 of the 6: Simple, Unexpected, Concrete, Credible, Emotional, Stories)
expectations.evidence: Section '7. It fails every SUCCESs criterion' explicitly lists all 6: Simple, Unexpected, Concrete, Credible, Emotional, Story, each with a brief evaluation.

expectations.text: References or applies the Curse of Knowledge concept
expectations.evidence: The output never mentions 'Curse of Knowledge' by name nor describes the concept. It discusses abstraction and insider assumptions but does not reference this specific Made to Stick concept.

expectations.text: Pushes concrete sensory language over abstract jargon
expectations.evidence: Section 3 states 'if I can't picture it, I can't remember it' and the closing recommends 'get specific, take a position, name a real person or moment, use language no competitor would use.'

expectations.text: Scores the tagline against the SUCCESs principles explicitly (not just generic critique)
expectations.evidence: Section 7 evaluates each of the 6 SUCCESs principles individually with specific judgments (e.g., 'Simple — 18 words, no core idea I can extract', 'Concrete — no image, no sensory detail, no specific case').

expectations.text: Flags abstract/jargon words like 'empowers', 'authentic', 'innovative solutions' as failures of Concrete/Simple
expectations.evidence: Section 2 individually flags 'empowers,' 'mission-driven,' 'amplify,' 'authentic voice,' 'innovative,' and 'storytelling solutions' as cliches, noting 'Six buzzwords in 18 words. That's a buzzword every three words.'
